review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to cryptography management. The naming is mostly clear and follows conventions, but a minor change was made to ensure consistency with existing patterns.
naming_improvements: Use 'enabled' instead of 'enforced' for consistency with existing naming patterns
title: Configuration Change Control | Cryptography Management
step1_reasoning: The control CM-3 (6) focuses on configuration change control with an emphasis on cryptography management. In Kubernetes, cryptography management can be mapped to ensuring that etcd data is encrypted at rest and that secrets are encrypted. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce cryptographic policies on resources being deployed. These checks align with the need to manage cryptographic configurations and ensure data protection.
